perturbation.original_text: Ownership: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: Ownership: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors, except for any modifications or improvements made by Recipient, which shall become the property of Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that any modifications or improvements made by the Recipient to the Confidential Information will become the property of the Recipient. This contradicts the initial statement that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosure and/or its licensors. This also creates uncertainty about what constitutes a 'modification or improvement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors, except for any modifications or improvements made by Recipient, which shall become the property of Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the ownership of Confidential Information. The original text states 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.' The changed text adds 'except for any modifications or improvements made by Recipient, which shall become the property of Recipient'. This creates a direct contradiction because now, not *all* Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure, which contradicts the original statement. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction exists within the same clause, but it's still a contradiction that was introduced.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other; however, Novell may terminate this agreement at anytime without written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by allowing Novell to terminate the agreement at any time without written notice, while requiring the other party to provide thirty (30) days written notice. This creates an imbalance in the termination rights and contradicts the initial statement that either party may terminate upon written notice. It makes it unclear if Silver Stream Software can also terminate without notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other; however, Novell may terminate this agreement at anytime without written notice. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows Novell to terminate the agreement at any time without written notice, while the original text and the contradicted text state that either party may terminate upon written notice. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as one party (Novell) now has a termination right that the other party does not, and it contradicts the original agreement that termination requires written notice from either party. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is provided "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. Discloser does not represent or warrant the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, that it will release any product related to the Confidential Information, or that target dates will be met.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information is provided "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. Discloser warrants the accuracy of financial projections shared within this confidential information. Discloser does not represent or warrant the completeness of the Confidential Information, that it will release any product related to the Confidential Information, or that target dates will be met.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that while all confidential information is provided "AS IS" without warranty, the discloser *does* warrant the accuracy of financial projections shared within this confidential information. This directly conflicts with the disclaimer of warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclaimer. All Confidential Information is provided "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. Discloser warrants the accuracy of financial projections shared within this confidential information. Discloser does not represent or warrant the completeness of the Confidential Information, that it will release any product related to the Confidential Information, or that target dates will be met. The entire risk arising out of the use of the Confidential Information remains with Recipient. Discloser may change or cancel its plans at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by adding a warranty for the accuracy of financial projections, which directly contradicts the initial statement that 'All Confidential Information is provided "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.' This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, since the Discloser is now both disclaiming and providing a warranty, and the contradicted text has the same conflicting language.

perturbation.original_text: In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.changed_text: In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees, unless the prevailing party is Novell, in which case each party bears its own fees.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stipulating that the prevailing party is entitled to legal fees, except when Novell prevails, contradicting the initial rule. This introduces uncertainty and unequal footing in potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees, unless the prevailing party is Novell, in which case each party bears its own fees. The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisdiction and venue in any such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original rule regarding attorney's fees, stating that if Novell prevails, each party bears its own fees. The contradicted text repeats the same rule with the same exception. While they are now the same, the change introduces an in-text contradiction to the original agreement. The original stated that the prevailing party shall be awarded fees and the changed text introduces an exception. Therefore the changed text caused a contradiction to the agreement that was later resolved by editing the contradicted text to be the same.
